signature.description: Base64url-encoded ECDSA P-256 signature (ASN.1 DER) over SHA-256(challenge)

request_id.description: Correlation identifier included in responses for request tracing.

interest_rate_bps.description: The current loan summary calculation treats this value as a percentage, so `24` means `24%`.

session_token.description: Present when status is new_device_detected because the submitted X-Device-ID was not an active trusted device; use this with OTP at /auth/verify-new-device or to request another code at /auth/resend-new-device-otp.
Session token returned from /auth/login when status is new_device_detected. The same token is also used with /auth/resend-new-device-otp.

email.description: Optional email address. When supplied, pair it with `email_verification_id`.

email_verification_id.description: Verification ID returned by `/auth/otp/verify` for the optional email step.

phone_number.description: Nigerian phone number. Accepted local or international forms are normalized server-side.

device_id.description: Same device identifier submitted in the original /auth/login request.

session_token.description: Session token returned from /auth/login when status is new_device_detected.

channel_id.description: Optional explicit Expo channel override. When omitted, the backend derives the channel from `type`.

device_id.description: Device identifier used during login initialization.

signature.description: Signature over challenge bytes from the device private key.

info.description:
HTTP API for authentication, OTP verification, device trust, identity validation,
password reset, and loan product workflows.
info.title: Neat Mobile App Backend API

post.description: Sends a password reset OTP to the account phone number for a recognized device.

parameters.description: Registered device identifier for the account.
parameters.name: X-Device-ID

400.description: Invalid request body

401.description: Account not found for phone number or device not recognized

500.description: Failed to create or send password reset OTP

post.description:
Authenticates a user by phone and password, then starts device verification flow.
The server evaluates the submitted `X-Device-ID` against the authenticated user's stored devices.
If that device exists and is both active and trusted, returns `challenge_required`.
If the device is missing, inactive, or untrusted, returns `new_device_detected`, sends a login OTP to the user's
phone number, and returns a `session_token` for `/auth/verify-new-device` and `/auth/resend-new-device-otp`.

parameters.description: Stable device installation identifier from the mobile app, used to look up device trust for the account.
parameters.name: X-Device-ID

200.description: Login initialized; response shape depends on device trust state

400.description: Invalid request body, invalid phone number, or missing/invalid device id

post.description: Revokes the current auth session and refresh token record.

400.description: Invalid request body or missing authorization header

post.description: Sends an OTP for login, signup, or password reset via SMS or email.

400.description: Invalid request body, purpose, channel, email, or phone number
Invalid request body, purpose, or channel

post.description: Validates a refresh token and device binding from request body, rotates the refresh token, and returns new access and refresh tokens.

400.description: Missing refresh token/device id or invalid request body

post.description: Creates a new account and binds the initial device in one transaction. The server captures the client IP from the request metadata and stores it with the created auth session.

400.description: Invalid request body or failed registration validation

post.description:
Resends the login OTP for an active `new_device_detected` session created by `/auth/login`.
The same `session_token` remains valid and the pending session expiry is extended.

400.description: Invalid request body or missing session token/device id

429.description: OTP resend cooldown or resend limit reached

post.summary: Resend OTP for new device login

post.description: Verifies a password reset OTP, marks it as used, and updates the user's password hash.

parameters.description: Registered device identifier for the account.
parameters.name: X-Device-ID

400.description: Invalid request body, missing reset code, or invalid password format

401.description: Invalid device id, reset code, or user context

post.description: Validates a BVN using Tendar only. Provider preference from CBA is ignored for this endpoint.

400.description: Missing or invalid request body, invalid BVN, or provider validation error

500.description: CBA lookup, provider, or internal error

post.description: Validates a NIN using the configured NIN provider and compares it with a previously verified BVN using `bvn_validation_id`.

400.description: Missing or invalid request body, invalid NIN, or provider validation error

post.description: Verifies a signed login challenge for a recognized trusted device and returns tokens.

400.description: Invalid request body or missing challenge fields

401.description: Invalid challenge or signature verification failed

post.description:
Completes login when the submitted `X-Device-ID` from `/auth/login` was not found as an
active trusted device, using the `session_token` returned by `/auth/login` and the OTP sent
during `new_device_detected` flow. If the OTP expires or is not received, request another code
with `/auth/resend-new-device-otp` using the same `session_token` and `device_id`.

200.description: New device verified and tokens issued

400.description: Invalid request body or missing required fields

401.description: Invalid/expired session token or invalid OTP

get.description:
Returns all loan applications with linked BVN records for internal CBA consumption.
This endpoint is served under `/internal/v1` and is protected by HMAC headers.

parameters.description: RFC3339 timestamp used in the HMAC signing string. Must be within five minutes of server time.
parameters.name: X-Timestamp

parameters.description:
Lowercase hex HMAC-SHA256 signature of `METHOD + "
" + PATH + "
" + TIMESTAMP + "
" + SHA256_HEX_OF_BODY` using `CBA_WEBHOOK_SECRET`.
parameters.name: X-Signature

401.description: Missing or invalid internal HMAC headers

500.description: Internal auth is not configured or the fetch failed

patch.description:
Callback endpoint used by CBA to update the local status of a loan application after review or booking.
This endpoint is served under `/internal/v1` and is protected by HMAC headers.

parameters.description: RFC3339 timestamp used in the HMAC signing string. Must be within five minutes of server time.
parameters.name: X-Timestamp

parameters.description:
Lowercase hex HMAC-SHA256 signature of `METHOD + "
" + PATH + "
" + TIMESTAMP + "
" + SHA256_HEX_OF_BODY` using `CBA_WEBHOOK_SECRET`.
parameters.name: X-Signature

400.description: Invalid request body, missing path value, or invalid status payload

401.description: Missing or invalid internal HMAC headers

500.description: Internal auth is not configured or the callback failed

patch.summary: Update loan application status from CBA

get.description: Returns the currently available loan products from storage.

post.description:
Creates a pending loan application for the authenticated user after validating
the selected product, business data, user verification state, and core-banking loan checks.

400.description: Invalid request body or malformed input fields

404.description: Current user or loan rule not found

409.description: Multiple core-banking customers matched the supplied BVN

422.description: User or application data failed business-rule validation

503.description: Core-banking integration is unavailable or not configured

get.description: Returns the repayment schedule for a specific core-banking loan id.

400.description: Missing or invalid loan id query parameter

500.description: Core-banking integration is unavailable or repayment lookup failed

get.description: Returns the authenticated user's in-app notification history in newest-first order.

parameters.description: Page number for paginated history retrieval.
parameters.name: page

parameters.description: Number of notifications to return per page.
parameters.name: page_size

500.description: Notification service is not configured or retrieval failed

patch.description: Marks all unread notifications for the authenticated user as read.

500.description: Notification service is not configured or update failed

post.description:
Internal endpoint used by trusted backend callers to create notification history, send Expo push messages,
and persist accepted Expo ticket ids for later receipt polling. This endpoint is served under `/internal/v1`
and is protected by HMAC headers.

parameters.description: RFC3339 timestamp used in the HMAC signing string.
parameters.name: X-Timestamp

parameters.description:
Lowercase hex HMAC-SHA256 signature of `METHOD + "
" + PATH + "
" + TIMESTAMP + "
" + SHA256_HEX_OF_BODY` using `NOTIFICATION_INTERNAL_SECRET`.
parameters.name: X-Signature

400.description: Invalid request body or missing required notification fields

401.description: Missing or invalid internal HMAC headers

delete.description: Deletes the push token associated with the authenticated user and the supplied `X-Device-ID`.

parameters.description: Device identifier used during token registration.
parameters.name: X-Device-ID

400.description: Missing device id header

500.description: Notification service is not configured or token deletion failed

post.description: Upserts the Expo push token for the authenticated user and device.

400.description: Invalid request body, platform, or Expo push token

500.description: Notification service is not configured or token registration failed

get.description: Returns the current unread notification count for the authenticated user.

500.description: Notification service is not configured or retrieval failed

patch.description: Marks a single notification owned by the authenticated user as read.

400.description: Missing notification id

500.description: Notification service is not configured or update failed

post.description:
Development-only endpoint served by `cmd/devsigner` to help Postman flows.
It signs `SHA-256(challenge)` using the configured ECDSA P-256 private key for `device_id`.

400.description: Invalid request body or missing fields

404.description: Device key not found in signer configuration
